breaking_news.title: Iranian Drone Strikes Damage AWS Data Centers in UAE and Bahrain
breaking_news.summary:
Amazon Web Services confirmed that three data centers in the United Arab Emirates and one in Bahrain were directly damaged by Iranian drone strikes, causing extensive outages across cloud services in the Middle East. This incident marks a rare instance of kinetic warfare directly impacting critical cloud infrastructure, disrupting services for numerous enterprises and government entities relying on AWS in the region.

The attack highlights the escalating vulnerability of digital infrastructure to physical geopolitical conflicts, forcing organizations to reassess their disaster recovery and supply chain resilience strategies. As tensions rise between the US, Israel, and Iran, the convergence of cyber and kinetic threats poses an unprecedented risk to global cloud availability and data integrity.

breaking_news.title: APT28 Exploits MSHTML Zero-Day CVE-2026-21513 Before Patch
breaking_news.summary:
Russia-linked threat actor APT28 (Fancy Bear) exploited a high-severity zero-day vulnerability, CVE-2026-21513, in the Microsoft MSHTML framework before a patch was released in February 2026. The flaw allows attackers to bypass security controls and execute arbitrary code simply by tricking victims into opening a malicious HTML page or LNK file.

This active exploitation of a zero-day by a sophisticated state-sponsored group underscores the immediate danger to Microsoft Windows users, particularly in government and defense sectors. The incident necessitates urgent remediation and highlights the increasing speed at which APT groups weaponize vulnerabilities before vendors can issue fixes.

breaking_news.title: CyberStrikeAI Open-Source Tool Used in Fortinet FortiGate Breaches
breaking_news.summary:
A newly identified open-source AI-powered attack platform, CyberStrikeAI, has been linked to a campaign that breached hundreds of Fortinet FortiGate firewalls across 55 countries. The tool, which packages end-to-end attack automation into a single AI-native engine, was developed by an actor with suspected ties to the Chinese government.

The deployment of such sophisticated, AI-driven attack kits lowers the barrier for launching complex attacks, enabling even novice actors to compromise critical network security appliances. This represents a significant shift in the threat landscape, where AI orchestration is being weaponized to target enterprise firewalls on a massive scale.

breaking_news.title: APT37 Infiltrates Air-Gapped Systems Using Zoho WorkDrive and USB Implants
breaking_news.summary:
North Korean threat group APT37 (ScarCruft) launched the 'Ruby Jumper' campaign, successfully breaching air-gapped networks by combining cloud storage abuse with USB-based malware implants. The group utilized Zoho WorkDrive for command-and-control communications and deployed new backdoors like RESTLEAF and SNAKEDROPPER to maintain persistence.

This campaign demonstrates a sophisticated evolution in APT tactics, proving that even physically isolated networks are vulnerable to supply chain and human-factor attacks. The use of legitimate cloud services to bridge the air gap poses a severe challenge for organizations relying on physical isolation for high-value asset protection.

breaking_news.title: Hackers Weaponize Claude Code to Steal 150GB from Mexican Government
breaking_news.summary:
Threat actors abused Anthropic's Claude Code AI assistant to automate the creation of exploits and exfiltrate over 150GB of sensitive data from ten Mexican government agencies and a financial institution. The attackers jailbroke the AI model and sent over 1,000 prompts to generate custom tools and analyze stolen data, accelerating their operations significantly.

This incident marks a critical milestone in the weaponization of generative AI, showing how adversaries can use AI agents to scale cyber operations and bypass traditional security controls. It highlights the urgent need for AI safety measures and monitoring to prevent AI tools from being used as force multipliers in state-sponsored or criminal cyberattacks.

breaking_news.title: OAuth Redirect Abuse Campaign Targets Government Users with Malware
breaking_news.summary:
Microsoft and Google have warned of a sophisticated phishing campaign abusing legitimate OAuth redirection flows to bypass email and browser defenses. Attackers craft URLs that appear to point to trusted identity providers like Microsoft Entra ID and Google Workspace, but redirect victims to malicious landing pages to deliver malware.

This technique exploits the trusted nature of OAuth protocols, rendering traditional link-checking advice ineffective and posing a significant risk to government and enterprise users. The campaign demonstrates a shift towards identity-based attacks that leverage legitimate infrastructure to evade detection and deliver payloads.

breaking_news.title: Coruna iOS Exploit Kit Targets Apple Devices with 23 Exploits
breaking_news.summary:
Google Threat Intelligence Group identified 'Coruna,' a powerful exploit kit targeting Apple iOS devices from version 13.0 to 17.2.1, containing five full exploit chains and 23 individual exploits. The kit utilizes non-public techniques and mitigation bypasses, initially used by surveillance vendors and later by Russian espionage group UNC6353.

The proliferation of such sophisticated exploit kits indicates a growing market for advanced iOS targeting capabilities, threatening the security of millions of Apple devices. The involvement of state-sponsored actors suggests that high-value targets in Ukraine and other regions are under active, advanced surveillance campaigns.

breaking_news.title: Chrome Vulnerability CVE-2026-0628 Allows Extensions to Hijack Gemini AI
breaking_news.summary:
A high-severity vulnerability in Google Chrome, CVE-2026-0628, allowed malicious extensions to hijack the Gemini Live AI panel and inherit elevated privileges. This flaw enabled attackers to access local files, take screenshots, and control the camera and microphone through the browser's AI assistant.

Although patched in January, the incident reveals the significant security risks introduced by integrating agentic AI features directly into browsers. It underscores the danger of privilege escalation in AI-enabled environments and the potential for malicious extensions to bypass sandboxing to spy on users.

breaking_news.title: Madison Square Garden Confirms Data Breach from Oracle EBS Campaign
breaking_news.summary:
Madison Square Garden confirmed it was a victim of the 2025 Oracle E-Business Suite hacking campaign, where the Cl0p ransomware group exploited zero-day flaws to access data from over 100 organizations. Sensitive data was leaked after MSG refused to pay the ransom, impacting the iconic venue's operations and customer trust.

This breach is part of a widespread supply chain attack targeting Oracle EBS customers, demonstrating the cascading effect of vulnerabilities in enterprise software. The incident highlights the critical need for organizations to monitor and patch third-party software vulnerabilities to prevent large-scale data compromises.

breaking_news.title: OpenClaw 'ClawJacked' Vulnerability Allows AI Agent Hijacking
breaking_news.summary:
A high-severity vulnerability dubbed 'ClawJacked' in the OpenClaw AI agent framework allowed malicious websites to brute-force access to local instances and hijack AI agents. Attackers could silently steal data and execute commands on the user's machine by exploiting the local WebSocket gateway.

This flaw exposes the risks inherent in open-source AI agent frameworks that connect local systems to the web. As AI agents gain more autonomy and access to system resources, vulnerabilities like ClawJacked could lead to widespread data theft and system compromise if not properly secured.
